title: image sign
body: Hello, I want to use cosign to sign my private harbor registry image $ cosign generate-key-pair $ podman image trust set --type signedBy --pubkeysfile /harbor/cosign/cosign.pub harbor.poc.test.com $podman image trust set -t reject default $ podman image trust show [root@harbor ~]# podman pull harbor.poc.test.com/library/nginx:v10.3 --log-level debug INFO[0000] podman harbor.poc.test.com/library/nginx:v10.3: Source image rejected: A signature was required, but no signature exists Error: Source image rejected: A signature was required, but no signature exists

comments.body: To use cosgin local signature，how to configure /etc/containers/registries.d/ and /etc/containers/policy.json ？

- Set `use-sigstore-attachments` in `registries.d/*.yaml` for the relevant repo
- Use `sigstoreSignedBy` (not `signedBy)` in `policy.json`; either manually, or with `podman image trust set` with a new enough Podman that accepts that.
- For Cosign operability, use `signedIdentity` `matchRepository`, and probably sign with `cosign sign -r`.

https://github.com/containers/image/issues/1613 has a working example.
Closing as I consider the question to be answered.  Feel free to continue the conversation.
